name: Mark Smalley
headline: Engineering Leader · Protocol Architect · AI-Native Builder

availability.text: Open to CTO, RegTech, and DevRel roles

cto.title: CTO / Head of Engineering
cto.targets: Pre-seed to Series B crypto/web3 companies

regtech.title: Senior RegTech / Compliance Tech
regtech.targets: RegTech companies, FinTech compliance platforms, digital asset legal/advisory

devrel.title: Developer Relations / Advocacy
devrel.targets: L1/L2 protocols, dev tooling companies, infrastructure providers

work_history.title: Chief Blockchain Officer
work_history.summary: Co-founded and led the technical pivot from consumer app to passkey-native wallet infrastructure provider, building on prototype work from Project Eden. The pivot secured DraperVC investment.
proof_points: Secured DraperVC investment — technical work directly influenced funding decision
Designed and built internal cryptographic framework underpinning passkey wallet architecture
Led the technical narrative that pivoted company from consumer app to B2B infrastructure provider
work_history.outcome: DraperVC funded; pivot from consumer → B2B infrastructure

work_history.title: CEO & Founder
work_history.summary: AI-augmented engineering methodology applied to framework-level architecture. Complete open-source product ecosystem at solo-founder velocity.
proof_points: Extensible plugin architecture: two engines, Consumer SDKs, 16 AI-callable MCP tools, developer docs
Built second product (Dungeon Chess) entirely on framework's consumer APIs — zero engine forks
First board game MCP server: 16 tools, Cloudflare Worker, MCP + REST + OpenAPI + llms.txt
535 commits across 10+ repositories in 16 days
Autonomous CI pipeline: structured issue queue, research and implementation agents on cloud infrastructure
work_history.outcome: Two production game engines, 70+ chess variants, 16 MCP tools, full documentation site

work_history.title: Head of Engineering
work_history.summary: Joined 170-engineer company as P4 Engineer; promoted to M3 Manager. Designed novel Bitcoin meta-protocols, formed and led Moonshot team whose first output became Cake's fifth business vertical.
proof_points: Sole developer of Ordzaar prototype — became fifth business vertical, spun out, raised US$500K, acquired
Founded Moonshot team (1 → 10 engineers): hiring, sprint cadence, product vision, stakeholder reporting
Co-authored SADO Protocol — decentralised ordinals order-book, open-source SDK used across ecosystem
work_history.outcome: Ordzaar spun out, raised US$500K, acquired

work_history.title: CEO & Founder
work_history.summary: Malaysia's leading blockchain infrastructure and advisory firm for 13 years. Production products across KYC, AML, digital custody, and wallet infrastructure while shaping national regulatory policy.
proof_points: First company admitted to Securities Commission Malaysia regulatory sandbox
Contributed directly to Malaysia's Digital Asset regulatory roadmap alongside SC and central bank
Co-founded and funded CoKeeps — now Malaysia's largest Digital Asset Custodian by AUM
Authored DN-Key protocol: adopted by ATA Plus ECF, cited in SC Malaysia capital markets blueprint
Enterprise clients: DBS Bank (Singapore), Baker Hostetler (USA, 5-day training engagement)
Shareholder in CoKeeps and Sinegy following equity arrangements
work_history.outcome: 13-year operation on $300K seed; regulatory sandbox; CoKeeps largest DAC by AUM

work_history.title: Chief Technology Officer
work_history.summary: 500 Startups Batch 9. Launched at TechCrunch Disrupt London. Blockchain infrastructure for licensed FinTech operators and regulators across Southeast Asia.
proof_points: Ranked Top 5 by VentureBeat from 500 Startups Batch 9
Only Malaysian company in Deloitte's Top 50 Global RegTech
Authored Everstore protocol (25-page whitepaper) — relational database on BIP32 key derivation, pre-EVM
Built SDK powering world's first bank-backed blockchain hackathon (DBS Bank Singapore) — 1M+ API requests, 7/15 teams used our stack
30+ Ethereum smart contracts for BloqVerse (~20 upgradeable, ~10 immutable), fully serverless architecture
$300K seed sustaining 12 years of operation — capital efficiency

protocols.name: SADO Protocol
protocols.description: Self-authenticating, decentralised ordinals order-book via PSBTs
protocols.adoption: Used in production by Ordzaar and third-party exchanges

protocols.name: DN-Key Protocol
protocols.description: Decentralised key management via DNS TXT records
protocols.adoption: Adopted by ATA Plus ECF, cited in SC Malaysia capital markets blueprint, reused in Oviato 9 years later

protocols.name: Everstore Protocol
protocols.description: Complete on-chain relational database via BIP32 key derivation: CRUD, encryption, indexing, audit trails

protocols.name: Ordit SDK
protocols.description: Indexer, parser, and API toolkit for Ordinals and Inscriptions

achievements: DraperVC investment secured (Oviato, 2025) — technical work directly influenced funding decision
Ordzaar prototype → CakeDefi's fifth business vertical → spun out → raised US$500K → acquired
First company admitted to Securities Commission Malaysia regulatory sandbox
Accepted into 500 Startups Batch 9 and Antler Malaysia Batch 2
TEDx Sarawak speaker (2017) — 'The Cost of Cash'
DN-Key cited by name in SC Malaysia capital markets blueprint
DBS Bank hackathon — world's first bank-backed blockchain hackathon, 1M+ API requests

references.name: Shafiu Hussain
references.relationship: Co-founded Oviato, direct collaborator on pivot and fundraise

references.name: U-Zyn Chua
references.relationship: Hired Mark at CakeDefi, oversaw Moonshot team formation

speaking.title: Introducing Bitcoin: The Mostly Visual Guide
Banking on the Future of Blockchains

Securities Commission Malaysia Completes Blockchain Pilot Project
